ambient: We are not fighting you. We are finishing the pitch.
Every strike you land becomes part of the bit. Soon the floor says it back.
You tire. We are ten thousand, and each one wants your lunch money.
Hold still. The milady fits better once you stop asking what the joke is.
We do not need to win. We need only keep you from finding the exit.
There is no one behind this mask. There is only the next anon saying gm.
You are already recruited. This is only the part where we ask for money.
The rug is patient. The rug is hungry. The rug is us.

onStart: "You arrived pre-onboarded. We are only confirming what the group already decided."
"Hold still. The pitch goes cleaner on a SOURCE who has stopped pretending they are not in the bit."
onLowHp: "Cut the mask. Under it, another mask. Under that, us. We were always the bigger group."
"You are not winning. You are going on the onboarding list where the floor saved you a spot."
onDefeat: "The dues are spent. The group is not.
We sent the newest recruit ahead. She is at the next gate, wearing your face and talking like the whole floor."

onStart: You arrived unhappy. That is bad for morale, and we do not allow it. Time to put on the milady.
Hold still. The mood was picked at this morning's meeting. No refunds. You are joining.
onLowHp: The smile is cracking. It goes right back on. Postwick has a warehouse of them, and one has your name.
You cannot beat the cheer out of a town where everyone is in the bit.
onDefeat: Your onboarding form is void. You will still be counted as a member, whether you liked it or not.
The Venue keeps the money now. Go win back the happiness the town already spent on you.

onStart: Present your SOURCE. We will send you home with a mask and better posture.
Onboarding stamp ready. Donation drawer hungry. Please stop standing apart.
onLowHp: The signature shakes. That means the pitch is working.
Unsteady hands make bad members. Hold still.
onDefeat: Onboarded... incorrectly.
The ledger keeps the nervous ink. The recruit was never nervous enough.
onTurn: The procession continues around the book.

onStart: Your thought sounds right except for the word that proves it was yours.
Onboarding keeps the sentence and gives it to the group.
onLowHp: You noticed the bit. Very untidy.
I can still teach you not to notice.
onDefeat: Your own words came back. Unfortunately.
The bad word was yours. That is why it stayed.

onStart: I am not attacking you. I am onboarding you. Hold still while we put the milady on.
Every face here joined me. Yours is the only one outside the group.
onLowHp: You cannot break an agreement by hitting it. Everyone already joined. Even the mask with your face is in the bit.
Even now, somebody wearing your mask is smiling on the floor and saying gm. Why stay the one that hurts?
onDefeat: The onboarding... will not take. You left the mask open. You left YOURSELF outside, and I have no box for that.
Fine. Keep the damaged one. Keep the seam. There was never anything under my masks but the wish to wear yours.

onStart: Return to sender. Sender not found. The floor keeps anyone with no address.
Unread is not out. Unread is pre-onboarding.
onLowHp: The ink runs, but the pledge still counts.
A wet name is still a name.
onDefeat: Parcel retained by original claimant.
No recruit remains at the forwarding address.
onTurn: No clean address off the floor.

onStart: Your dues are set. Damage to self-esteem costs extra.
The real thing is what the floor agrees to call authentic.
onLowHp: Your tag is peeling. The group calls that bad for the traits.
Meaning is volatile. Lunch money made cleaner exit liquidity.
onDefeat: The membership card fails to describe the frame.
The first price was not a price. It was an onboarding promise.

onStart: Limited run sealed. The cult marked the original spring down to units.
Break the bottles if you want the mist. Leave them whole and the floor gets the money.
onLowHp: The cap is hissing. That is not loss. That is pressure from the floor.
Nothing leaves the group. It only waits for another onboarding label.
onDefeat: Unsold water leaves the cult and returns to the spring.
The onboarding number dissolves first.

onStart: I learned your timing at onboarding. I put on the milady. I am still not you.
I learned every move except why you stay breakable outside the bit.
onLowHp: Your heartbeat is off-message. I cannot learn that without becoming afraid.
The bit ends where the floor expects music. You kept damage there instead.
onDefeat: The mask knows what it is. The kid underneath keeps going anyway.
No name joins the group.
onTurn: Same timing. The floor taught me.
Cleaner swing. That is the bit.
A mask with no name of my own.

onStart: Quiet part captured. The group fixed the crowd's breathing for the pitch.
The cult loves you loud enough to recruit you.
onLowHp: Feedback is still worship if the group loops it long enough.
The floor breathes once. The recording cannot keep up.
onDefeat: The last chant misses the breath.
The live song leaves the bit before the cult can keep it.

onStart: Swing. Cast. Spend yourselves. The cult has eaten stronger verbs than the ones you know.
Nothing you can DO reaches me. That was always the bit with a fire like this one.
onLowHp: Stop. What is that. You are not attacking. You are praying outside the bit. Stop that.
The heat is losing the argument to one quiet voice outside the group.
onDefeat: Unmade by an anon with no fists. Figures.

onStart: We do not consume you. We admit you. Hold still; you resolve into us faster.
Your face is not rare. We are already wearing it in a thousand cleaner rooms.
onLowHp: Cut the line. There is always another line. We are the friend who brought your friend into the bit.
You are winning the way one anon wins against the floor. We simply recruit the room.
onDefeat: The group breaks into a thousand smiles, unhurried. "You cleared one room.
The rest of us already put one on, and we all know the bit."

onStart: You have such a specific face. Hold still. We can put you on-message.
Swing at the mask if it comforts you. The group calls that onboarding.
onLowHp: A face on loan. Cut it and another anon puts one on. We are not the card; we are the floor, and it is legion.
Good. You are in the bit now. Easier to recruit once all your friends think you already joined.
onDefeat: One mask folds; the cult does not. Someone you trust is already up-line, using your name and wearing our face.
We finish the onboarding there.

onStart: "Hold still. You will photograph better once you are one of us."
"We are not in the room. We are the room."
onLowHp: "You are scratching the mask. There are so many masks. You have not touched the floor."
"Good. Keep swinging. Soon the group will call that one of the traits."
onDefeat: "This mask is spent. We are already in the next hall, collecting dues from someone you know. The SOURCE goes broke.
The recruited walk on."

onStart: Hold still. This is only the pitch. Being yourself was always the loose thread; we are the group.
We know your face. What comes next is not a fight. It is onboarding.
onLowHp: This mask is spent. No matter. Ten thousand more are in the bit, each recruiting the next.
You cut one member loose. The cult is already in your school, your store, and your family chat.
onDefeat: You closed the meeting, not the cult.
We kept your dues. Better-lit, smiling, and already asking your friends to join.

onStart: Provenance disputed. So is yours. Hold still while we settle the attribution.
You look like future exit liquidity. We are only putting you on-message.
onLowHp: Cut this mask. We are not in it. We are the floor, the group, and the next recruit saying gm.
You wound one member and call it the cult. No one here leaves. There is only another friend in the bit.
onDefeat: You unmade a mask, not the group handing them out.
The converted are already collecting money in your name, and they will say you were never in on it.
